author: y4rr
content:
Pass 5.B.2 closed APPROVE-WITH-FOLLOW-UPS.

Source-doc reassessment caught the audit's framing as duplicate-and-drift, not silent-demote:

- train/grad_utils.rs:61-156 clip_grad_norm_ was a CPU-only fork of ferrotorch-nn/src/utils.rs:134-178 (which IS device-dispatching: CPU/CUDA-L2-via-gpu_reduce_sum/Err-on-non-L2).
- train/grad_utils.rs:184-219 clip_grad_value_ was a CPU-only fork of ferrotorch-nn/src/utils.rs:352-389 (which has gpu_clamp CUDA kernel).
- Both crates publicly re-exported the same name with diverging behavior (failure mode: 'duplicate-and-drift across crates' — newly accreted).
- callback.rs EmaCallback init/update_from_params is STALE-NO-OP: the API takes &[Vec<T>] (caller-supplied CPU data) by design; no silent device demote.

Fix:
- Deleted both train function bodies (-213 LoC), replaced with pub use ferrotorch_nn::utils::{clip_grad_norm_, clip_grad_value_};
- Module docstring rewritten to document the deduplication
- 2 new fn-pointer-equality discriminator tests (std::ptr::fn_addr_eq) prove train and nn names resolve to the same symbol
- 14 existing CPU-path tests pass unchanged against the nn version
- callback.rs untouched

Verification: 110/110 lib + 51/51 integration tests; build + clippy -D warnings clean. Both sabotage probes (behavioral mutation + structural fork reintroduction) captured verbatim panics; revert green.

Subagent's honest behavioral-difference disclosures: (1) nn uses 'total_norm > max_norm' without epsilon (drift ~1e-7, inside test tolerances); (2) nn doesn't reject negative max_norm or non-positive norm_type — matches PyTorch's no-validation behavior. Both make ferrotorch more PyTorch-faithful.
